parameters.text: =✅ Build a weekly summary with PostgreSQL and Discord on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Source Feed

### Read Source Feed

Pulls the RSS feed and emits one item per entry (title, link, content).

Ports: ①→ Skip Already-Seen Posts · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Skip Already-Seen Posts

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Keep Top Stories

### Keep Top Stories

Caps the stream at 5 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

### Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Ports: ①→ Publish to Discord, Publish to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Rewrite for Our Audience (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Publish to Discord

discord — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Confirm Cross-Post · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Publish to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Confirm Cross-Post · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Confirm Cross-Post

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Log Published Posts (PostgreSQL)

### Log Published Posts (PostgreSQL)

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Tag Pipeline Metadata · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Sort Events Newest First

### Sort Events Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (PostgreSQL)

### Append Audit Row (PostgreSQL)

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Build a weekly summary with PostgreSQL and Discord on autopilot

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Build a weekly summary with PostgreSQL and Discord on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual escalations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Source Feed → Skip Already-Seen Posts → Keep Top Stories → Rewrite for Our Audience (AI) → Publish to Discord → Confirm Cross-Post → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating escalations in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Discord webhook URL; OpenRouter API key; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your escalations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
